  "text": "func TestTokenRevocationJob_Run(t *testing.T) {
	// t.Parallel() - this was causing test failures, investigate before un-commenting
n1: 	ctx := context.Background()
n2: 	assert, require := assert.New(t), require.New(t)

n3: 	conn, _ := db.TestSetup(t, "postgres")
n4: 	rw := db.New(conn)
n5: 	wrapper := db.TestWrapper(t)
n6: 	kmsCache := kms.TestKms(t, conn, wrapper)
n7: 	org, prj := iam.TestScopes(t, iam.TestRepo(t, conn, wrapper))
n8: 	v := NewTestVaultServer(t, WithDockerNetwork(true))
n9: 	v.MountDatabase(t)

n10: 	_, ct := v.CreateToken(t)
n11: 	in, err := NewCredentialStore(prj.GetPublicId(), v.Addr, []byte(ct))
n12: 	require.NoError(err)
n13: 	sche := scheduler.TestScheduler(t, conn, wrapper)
n14: 	repo, err := NewRepository(ctx, rw, rw, kmsCache, sche)
n15: 	require.NoError(err)

n16: 	j, err := newTokenRenewalJob(ctx, rw, rw, kmsCache)
n17: 	require.NoError(err)
n18: 	err = sche.RegisterJob(ctx, j)
n19: 	require.NoError(err)

n20: 	cs, err := repo.CreateCredentialStore(ctx, in)
n21: 	require.NoError(err)

n22: 	r, err := newTokenRevocationJob(ctx, rw, rw, kmsCache)
n23: 	require.NoError(err)

n24: 	err = sche.RegisterJob(ctx, r)
n25: 	require.NoError(err)

	// No tokens should have been revoked since only the current token exists
n26: 	err = r.Run(ctx)
n27: 	require.NoError(err)
n28: 	assert.Equal(0, r.numProcessed)

	// Create maintaining tokens with and without credentials
n29: 	noCredsToken := testVaultToken(t, conn, wrapper, v, cs, MaintainingToken, 5*time.Minute)
n30: 	credsToken := testVaultToken(t, conn, wrapper, v, cs, MaintainingToken, 5*time.Minute)
n31: 	revokeToken := testVaultToken(t, conn, wrapper, v, cs, RevokeToken, 5*time.Minute)

	// inserting new tokens moves the current token to a maintaining state, move it back to current and set expiration time
n32: 	count, err := rw.Exec(ctx, testUpdateTokenStatusExpirationQuery, []any{CurrentToken, (5 * time.Minute).Seconds(), cs.outputToken.TokenHmac})
n33: 	require.NoError(err)
n34: 	assert.Equal(1, count)

	// Create cred lib and session for credential
n35: 	libPath := path.Join("database", "creds", "opened")
n36: 	cl, err := NewCredentialLibrary(cs.PublicId, libPath, WithMethod(MethodGet))
n37: 	require.NoError(err)
n38: 	cl.PublicId, err = newCredentialLibraryId(ctx)
n39: 	require.NoError(err)
n40: 	err = rw.Create(ctx, cl)
n41: 	require.NoError(err)

n42: 	at := authtoken.TestAuthToken(t, conn, kmsCache, org.GetPublicId())
n43: 	uId := at.GetIamUserId()
n44: 	hc := static.TestCatalogs(t, conn, prj.GetPublicId(), 1)[0]
n45: 	hs := static.TestSets(t, conn, hc.GetPublicId(), 1)[0]
n46: 	h := static.TestHosts(t, conn, hc.GetPublicId(), 1)[0]
n47: 	static.TestSetMembers(t, conn, hs.GetPublicId(), []*static.Host{h})
n48: 	tar := tcp.TestTarget(ctx, t, conn, prj.GetPublicId(), "test", target.WithHostSources([]string{hs.GetPublicId()}))
n49: 	target.TestCredentialLibrary(t, conn, tar.GetPublicId(), cl.GetPublicId())
n50: 	sess := session.TestSession(t, conn, wrapper, session.ComposedOf{
		UserId:      uId,
		HostId:      h.GetPublicId(),
		TargetId:    tar.GetPublicId(),
		HostSetId:   hs.GetPublicId(),
		AuthTokenId: at.GetPublicId(),
		ProjectId:   prj.GetPublicId(),
		Endpoint:    "tcp://127.0.0.1:22",
	})

	// Create credential attached to credsToken
n51: 	_, cred := testVaultCred(t, conn, v, cl, sess, credsToken, ActiveCredential, 5*time.Minute)

	// Create fake credential attached to revokeToken
n52: 	_, revokeCred := testVaultCred(t, conn, v, cl, sess, revokeToken, ActiveCredential, 5*time.Minute)

	// Running should revoke noCredsToken and the revokeToken even though it has active
	// credentials it has been marked for revocation
n53: 	err = r.Run(ctx)
n54: 	require.NoError(err)
n55: 	assert.Equal(2, r.numProcessed)

	// Verify noCredsToken was revoked in vault
n56: 	v.VerifyTokenInvalid(t, string(noCredsToken.GetToken()))

	// Verify noCredsToken was set to revoked in repo
n57: 	repoToken := allocToken()
n58: 	require.NoError(rw.LookupWhere(ctx, &repoToken, "token_hmac = ?", []any{noCredsToken.TokenHmac}))
n59: 	assert.Equal(string(RevokedToken), repoToken.Status)

	// Verify revokeToken was revoked in vault
n60: 	v.VerifyTokenInvalid(t, string(revokeToken.GetToken()))

	// Verify revokeToken was set to revoked in repo
n61: 	repoToken = allocToken()
n62: 	require.NoError(rw.LookupWhere(ctx, &repoToken, "token_hmac = ?", []any{revokeToken.TokenHmac}))
n63: 	assert.Equal(string(RevokedToken), repoToken.Status)

	// Verify revokeCred attached to revokeToken were marked as revoked
n64: 	lookupCred := allocCredential()
n65: 	lookupCred.PublicId = revokeCred.PublicId
n66: 	require.NoError(rw.LookupById(ctx, lookupCred))
n67: 	assert.Equal(string(RevokedCredential), lookupCred.Status)

	// Verify credsToken was not revoked in vault
n68: 	lookupToken := v.LookupToken(t, string(credsToken.GetToken()))
n69: 	assert.NotNil(lookupToken)

	// Revoke credential in repo
n70: 	query, queryValues := cred.updateStatusQuery(RevokedCredential)
n71: 	rows, err := rw.Exec(ctx, query, queryValues)
n72: 	assert.Equal(1, rows)
n73: 	assert.NoError(err)

	// Running again should now revoke the credsToken
n74: 	err = r.Run(ctx)
n75: 	require.NoError(err)
n76: 	assert.Equal(1, r.numProcessed)

	// Verify credsToken was revoked in vault
n77: 	v.VerifyTokenInvalid(t, string(credsToken.GetToken()))

	// Verify credsToken was set to revoked in repo
n78: 	repoToken = allocToken()
n79: 	require.NoError(rw.LookupWhere(ctx, &repoToken, "token_hmac = ?", []any{credsToken.TokenHmac}))
n80: 	assert.Equal(string(RevokedToken), repoToken.Status)

n81: 	err = r.Run(ctx)
n82: 	require.NoError(err)
	// With only the current token remaining no tokens should be revoked
n83: 	assert.Equal(0, r.numProcessed)
}
"

part.text: I’m unable to identify the full scope of job types and their Run methods. To make a consistent change across the codebase, I would need to enumerate every job struct that implements a Run method, update each signature to include an additional `time.Duration` parameter, and adjust all call sites to pass a zero value. This involves a large number of edits across multiple packages and test files, which is beyond the current interactive capacity.
